npm run build
npm ci --omit=dev
 // zip -r ../backend-lambda.zip dist node_modules package.json
Compress-Archive -Path dist,node_modules,package.json -DestinationPath ..\backend-lambda.zip -Force
npm install


Lambda
Create function
Author from scratch

Code - tab
Upload from - .zip
Runtime settings
Handler: dist/lambda.handler


Configuration
General configuration
Edit
Timeout: 10 sec
Memory: 256 MB


Test
Create new event



------------------------------------------------------------
API Gateway → APIs → Create API → under HTTP API → Build.
Add integration - Lambda - pick your region and function
Configure routes: Method ANY, path /{proxy+} → Next
Configure stages: keep $default with Auto-deploy on → Next → Create
Invoke URL



IAM - Users - Create user - Name - github-actions-deployer
Attach policies directly → Create inline policy → JSON tab:

{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": "lambda:UpdateFunctionCode",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:<region>:<account-id>:function:cognito-demo-backend"
  }]
}

Policy Name


Create the user → open it → Security credentials → Create access key → use case "Application running outside AWS" → copy the Access key ID and Secret access key now (shown once).

2. Add secrets to GitHub

Repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret, four times:

Secret	Value
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID	from step 1
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY	from step 1
AWS_REGION	e.g. us-east-1
LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME	cognito-demo-backend
